plan: Create `src/signals/types.ts` with a `DeviceCapabilitySignals` interface defining typed fields: `screenWidth: number | string`, `screenHeight: number | string`, `devicePixelRatio: number | string`, `hardwareConcurrency: number | string`, `touchSupport: boolean | string`, `storageSupport: { localStorage: boolean | string; sessionStorage: boolean | string; indexedDB: boolean | string }`. Use string fallback type for unavailable values.

Create `src/signals/device-signals.ts` exporting a pure function `collectDeviceCapabilitySignals(): DeviceCapabilitySignals` that collects all required signals using feature detection (`typeof window !== 'undefined'`, property existence checks) before accessing browser APIs. Return safe fallback string `'unavailable'` for any unsupported API.
Implement screen dimensions collection: read `window.screen.width` and `window.screen.height` with feature detection guard.
Implement device pixel ratio collection: read `window.devicePixelRatio` with feature detection, fallback to `'unavailable'`.
Implement hardware concurrency collection: read `navigator.hardwareConcurrency` with feature detection, fallback to `'unavailable'`.
Implement touch support detection: check `'ontouchstart' in window` or `navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0`, fallback to `'unavailable'`.

Implement storage support detection: test `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, and `indexedDB` availability via try/catch feature detection (some browsers throw on access in private mode), return boolean per storage type or `'unavailable'` fallback.

Create `tests/us-007/device-signals.test.ts` with Vitest tests covering: (a) function returns correct interface shape, (b) all required fields present, (c) values are correct types (number or fallback string), (d) fallback behavior when APIs are unavailable (mock `window`/`navigator` to simulate missing APIs).
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to validate all acceptance criteria pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test that `collectDeviceCapabilitySignals()` returns an object with all required keys: screenWidth, screenHeight, devicePixelRatio, hardwareConcurrency, touchSupport, storageSupport
Unit test that each numeric field returns a number in a normal browser environment (via jsdom/happy-path)
Unit test fallback behavior by mocking/deleting browser globals (e.g., delete window.screen) and asserting the function returns 'unavailable' strings instead of throwing
Unit test storage detection handles try/catch scenarios (e.g., localStorage throws SecurityError in private browsing)
Unit test that the return type matches the DeviceCapabilitySignals interface (TypeScript compilation is the test)
Run `npm run lint` — no violations in new files
Run `npm run typecheck` — no type errors
Run `npm run test` — all tests pass
risks: Vitest uses jsdom/happy-dom which may not fully support all browser APIs (e.g., `window.screen`, `navigator.hardwareConcurrency`). Tests may need to mock these globals rather than relying on the test environment providing them.
Storage detection via try/catch may behave differently across test environments vs real browsers — ensure fallback paths are tested with mocked throwing accessors.

If US-006 (locale signals) is implemented concurrently, both tickets create `src/signals/types.ts` — coordinate to avoid merge conflicts by keeping type definitions modular or combining into a shared types file.
